branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Electroweak / vacuum-anchor QFT observable; vacuum-projection side

description: Fisher's combined-p statistic for the Yukawa-Damping cross-sector cluster: three independent observables (alpha_dn flavor exponent, w_DE dark-energy equation of state, H_0 cosmological expansion rate) all close on the single loop-class factor 1+gamma/4 (Lemma 1, Yukawa-Damping). Each per-observable null tail probability is computed from the residual under a uniform prior over the 9-class loop-factor library (Lemmas 1-8 plus pure-sync); Fisher's chi-square statistic combines the three independent tails into a joint significance. The 4.05 sigma headline of the abstract follows from this bundle.

observables.name: alpha_dn
observables.p_source: p_i = post_loop_residual / 2.5% (uniform-tail null on the residual cut)
observables.anchor_source: Yukawa flavor-exponent target from Paper 3 cross-sector closure

framing: The Yukawa-Damping cluster is a cross-sector empirical signature: three observables in two physically distinct sectors close on the same loop-class factor at sub-0.3% residuals. The Fisher's-combined p_combined = 2.6e-5 corresponds to ~4.05 sigma significance under the conservative uniform-prior null. The cluster is the empirical mechanism by which the deterministic loop-class mapping of Paper 3 is independently corroborated; the same three observables also anchor the cross-sector cosmological-anchor table of Paper 5 (data/cosmological_anchors.json).
